J205/02: National and international economics

Mark Scheme — Predicted Paper (Advanced)
Total marks: 80


                              Mark Scheme — Advanced | Page 1 of 14
                                                           J205/02 Mark Scheme | Predicted Paper — Advanced

                                     MARKING INSTRUCTIONS
Preparation for marking
• Read and understand the mark scheme and the question paper before marking.
• Mark strictly to the mark scheme; marks awarded must relate directly to the marking criteria.
• If in doubt about applying the mark scheme, consult your Team Leader.
• Where a candidate has crossed out a response and provided a clear alternative, the crossed-out
   response is not marked. Where no alternative is provided, examiners may give candidates the benefit of
   the doubt and mark the crossed-out response where legible.
Multiple-choice question responses (Section A)
• Where a candidate provides two responses (even if one is correct) no mark is awarded, as the first
  response cannot be determined.
• Contradictory responses receive no mark even if one is correct.
Levels of response (Section B extended items)
• Determine the level first: start at the highest level and work down until you reach the level that matches
  the answer.
• Determine the mark within the level using the best-fit approach: top of level = consistently meets the
  criteria; bottom of level = on the borderline of this level and the one below.
• For questions marked with an asterisk (*) Quality of Extended Response is assessed: credit is given for
  coherent, well-structured and logically sequenced reasoning.
Annotations
✓     Tick — point worthy of credit
✗     Cross — incorrect / not creditworthy
BOD Benefit of the doubt
IR    Irrelevant
L1 / L2 / L3     Level one / two / three
TV Too vague
NAQ Not answered question
SEEN             Noted but no credit given
OFR Own figure rule applied
BP Blank page

21 (d) (ii) State two limitations of real GDP as a measure of               2      1 mark for each valid
            living standards.                                             AO1a     limitation, up to 2 marks.
            1. Ignores the distribution of income / inequality
            2. Excludes non-market activity (unpaid work,
            household production, black market)
            3. Does not measure health, education, life
            expectancy, or well-being
            4. Does not capture environmental costs of growth
            5. Averages hide regional differences
            6. Does not fully capture quality improvements in
            goods and services
